readme:
# LeanAlgebraicGeometry 

LeanAG is a collection of Lean 4 / Mathlib formalization projects around algebraic geometry. Its central goal is the algebraic-geometry version of the [Algebraic Jacobian challenge](https://leanprover.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/583336-Autoformalization/topic/Jacobian.20challenge/near/587802685), proposed by Christian Merten after Kevin Buzzard's original differential-geometry challenge on Zulip.

The repository is organized as a scope rather than a single monolithic project. The main Jacobian formalization is developed alongside supporting subprojects and related-paper formalizations, so that reusable infrastructure can be shared across the whole effort.

You can consult the [dashboard](https://axeldlv00.github.io/LeanAlgebraicGeometry/) for a visual overview of each project's blueprint, code, progress, and dependency DAG.

See the [scope roadmap](roadmap.md) for a high-level overview of the scope's member projects and their dependencies.

For more details, run `archon dashboard <project-name>` for any project in the scope and consult the generated dashboard. It renders logs and diffs that cannot be shown on the static webpages.

## Table of Contents

- [Scope Map](#scope-map)
- [Methodology](#methodology)
- [How to Contribute ?](#how-to-contribute-)
- [License](#license)

## Scope Map

| Project | Role in the scope | Main dependency direction | Status (open `sorry`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | 
| `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` | Core Jacobian / Picard / curve geometry engine | Provides infrastructure for most downstream projects | üîÑ In progress (93) |
| `Cech-Cohomology` | ƒåech comparison and higher direct image machinery | Feeds back into the Jacobian challenge and related papers | ‚úÖ Complete (0) |
| `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso` | Tensor/dual comparison isomorphisms for relative Picard | Unblocks the Jacobian challenge at large scale | üîÑ In progress (2) |
| `Quot-Foundations` | Quot, Grassmannian, and flat-base-change foundations | Supports the secant-bundle paper formalization | üîÑ In progress (21) |
| `GR-quot_closure` | Grassmannian-quotient representability (H‚Å∞ leg) | Extracted from `Quot-Foundations`; merges back | üîÑ In progress (13) |
| `FBC-B_SNAP-chain` | Flat-base-change leg + shared SNAP foundation | Extracted from `Quot-Foundations`; merges back | üîÑ In progress (14) |
| 26 related-paper projects | Formalization of related algebraic-geometry papers | A few consume the AG base (secant/perverse/Mordell‚ÄìLang); most are self-contained | üìù Blueprint only (0 Lean) |

## Methodology

We use [Archon v0.3.0](https://github.com/frenzymath/Archon) to make progress on the formalization, with `Claude Opus 4.8` for critical steps and `Claude Sonnet` for routine tasks.

Archon is launched autonomously, human intervention is sometimes used as "hints" given occasionally as guidance. Creating subprojects or merging subprojects also requires human intervention, but Archon is used to generate the content of the subprojects and merge them afterwards.

The version of Archon used here allows projects to read from each other. This helps avoid duplicated work and makes development across the scope more organic.

## How to Contribute ?

Contributions are welcome and can take several forms.

Issues are a good place to propose new subprojects, suggest hints that should be given to Archon, report gaps, or comment on specific parts of the code. If your issue concerns a particular declaration, please mention the declaration name and file path, and explain whether the question is about the statement, the name, the proof strategy, or its intended use.

Pull requests are welcome for direct contributions to the formalization. You may use Archon or your own methods. Since Archon is currently running in most subprojects, it can be useful to open an issue before starting a larger contribution, or to work in a separate subproject that you created, or to choose a point in [the roadmap](./roadmap.md) that is marked `[ ]`, so that work is not duplicated.

When adding declarations, please make names as clear and stable as possible. A good name should indicate the mathematical content of the result, fit the surrounding namespace, and be easy to search for later. If a theorem is only a temporary helper, make that clear either from its local context or from a short comment/docstring.

We recommend using [Archon v0.3.0](https://github.com/frenzymath/Archon) if you run projects in this scope, because the project is structured to be compatible with Archon, but you may also use your own methods, with or without harness.

## License

This repository is distributed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE).

Author: Axel Delaval, AI4MATH@PKU
roadmap:
# LeanAG ‚Äî Scope Roadmap (condensed)

A high-level, mathematical checklist across the scope's member projects.

**Legend:**

- [x] proved / sorry-free (or, for a theme, its keystone declarations are sorry-free)
- [~] in progress (declarations exist, residual `sorry`)
- [ ] not started (no Lean yet ‚Äî blueprint only, or theme not begun)

**Status snapshot** *(open `sorry` counts measured over each project's `AlgebraicJacobian/`
source tree, comments/docstrings excluded; 2026-06-18):*

| Project | Stage | Open `sorry` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge | prover | 93 |
| Cech-Cohomology | ‚úÖ complete | 0 |
| Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso | prover | 2 |
| Quot-Foundations | prover | 21 |
| GR-quot_closure | prover | 13 |
| FBC-B_SNAP-chain | prover | 14 |
| 26 related-paper projects | üìù blueprint only | 0 Lean (stub aggregators) |

---

## Dependency spine

### Core algebraic-geometry engine

- `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso` ‚Üí `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (largest leverage: unblocks the Picard / comparison-iso substrate; merges back the `A.1.c.sub` package)
- `Cech-Cohomology` ‚Üî `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (the ƒåech `R‚Å±f_*` engine is the cohomological substrate; proved sorry-free here, **merged into the AJC tree** 2026-06-18)
- `GR-quot_closure`, `FBC-B_SNAP-chain` ‚Üí `Quot-Foundations` (extracted work packages of the Quot/Picard-representability cone; share the SNAP section-graded-ring foundation `Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean`)
- `Quot-Foundations` ‚Üí `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (the H‚Å∞ Picard-representability cone ‚Äî flat base change, Grassmannian, Quot ‚Äî merges back)

### Related papers that consume the AG base

These declare (or directly reference) infrastructure from the core engine. Only
`MR4213770` and `MR4228499` carry an explicit `*_PeerDependencies` blueprint chapter;
the rest reference the base informally.

- `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (H^i scheme cohomology), `Cech-Cohomology` (R‚Å±f_*), `Quot-Foundations` (flat base change, stalkwise module-iso criterion) ‚Üí **`MR4213770`** *(explicit PeerDependencies chapter)*
- `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (Jacobian, Weil divisors, symmetric powers C‚Åø, canonical differentials), `Cech-Cohomology` (R‚Å±f_*) ‚Üí **`MR4228499`** *(explicit PeerDependencies chapter)*
- `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (Jacobian, principal polarization, Torelli) ‚Üí **`MR4276287`** *(informal references)*
- AG base (spectral curves / Jacobians, geometric Satake, arc spaces ‚Äî not yet pinned to specific peers) ‚Üí **`MR4433079`** *(aspirational)*
- `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (abelian varieties, curve geometry) ‚Üí **`MR4665779`** *(conceptual; the blueprint is currently self-contained ‚Äî the earlier `MR4199442+MR4213770+MR4228499 ‚Üí MR4665779` paper-to-paper edges are **not** present in the blueprints and have been dropped)*

### Self-contained papers

The remaining 20 related-paper projects are **independent blueprint formalizations**: they
share the scope's conventions and tooling but declare **no dependency on the AG base**. See
the per-paper sections below.

---

## Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge  *(core engine ‚Äî prover stage, 93 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve ‚Äî smooth of
relative dimension = genus, proper, geometrically irreducible, and the Albanese variety
(`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`). Spine = pointed vs. unpointed; 0 project axioms.

- [x] **K√§hler-differential / cotangent substrate** ‚Äî `Cotangent/GrpObj`, `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra`, `Differentials` (cotangent iso, chart algebra) **sorry-free**
- [x] **Rigidity & Abel‚ÄìJacobi scaffolding** ‚Äî `Rigidity`, `RigidityLemma`, `Genus`, `AbelJacobi` **sorry-free**
- [x] **Line-bundle coherence substrate** ‚Äî `Picard/LineBundleCoherence`, `Picard/LineBundlePullback`, `Picard/RelPicFunctor`, `Picard/RelativeSpec` **sorry-free** (local triviality, pullback-tensor compatibility)
- [x] **ƒåech higher-direct-image engine (A.2.c)** ‚Äî the comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` and `pushPull` functoriality (`pushPullFunctor`, `pushPullMap_comp`) are **proved sorry-free in `Cech-Cohomology`** and merged in; `cechHigherDirectImage` is sorry-free in the AJC tree. *(The ƒåech theorem itself has no open mathematical gap.)*
- [~] **ƒåech merge clean-up & flat base change** ‚Äî the AJC copy still carries **9 `sorry`**, but these are **not** gaps in the ƒåech engine: 7 are **MERGE-STUBs** ‚Äî proofs proved upstream / preserved in-file, `sorry`-ed only to dodge build-time elaboration blow-ups during the merge (`CechSectionIdentificationLeg` √ó5, `CechToHigherDirectImage` √ó2), pending a clean restore ‚Äî and 2 are the genuinely-open **flat base change** consumer (Stacks 02KH: `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` in `FlatBaseChange`, `cech_flatBaseChange` in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) that *uses* the ƒåech infra
- [~] **Group schemes** ‚Äî `Ga`, `Gm`, `ProjectiveLineBar` (‚Ñô¬π) **defined**; `Genus0BaseObjects` carries **2 residual `sorry`** (`BareScheme`, `GmScaling`) *(was previously mismarked "not started")*
- [~] **Tensor/dual comparison substrate + Picard group (A.1.c.sub)** ‚Äî `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate` defines `PicGroup`/`picCommGroup` and the slice-dual transport; **3 residual `sorry`** *(shared with `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`)*
- [~] **Weil divisors & Riemann‚ÄìRoch core** ‚Äî order valuation, degree homomorphism, principal divisors, skyscraper SES (`RiemannRoch/*`, **15 `sorry`**: `RationalCurveIso`, `OcOfD`, `OCofP`, `WeilDivisor` √ó3 each; `H1Vanishing` √ó2; `RRFormula` √ó1)
- [~] **Albanese / abelian-variety leg** ‚Äî `Albanese/*` (**12 `sorry`**: `AlbaneseUP` √ó7, `CodimOneExtension` √ó3, `Thm32RationalMapExtension` √ó2; `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge` sorry-free)
- [~] **Picard representability cone** ‚Äî `Picard/QuotScheme` (√ó12), `IdentityComponent` (√ó9), `FGAPicRepresentability` (√ó7), `FlatteningStratification` (√ó7), `Pic0AbelianVariety` (√ó5) *(consumes the `Quot-Foundations` H‚Å∞ leg)*
- [~] **Flatness & generic flatness** ‚Äî flat-locus open ‚Üí Noetherian stratification (`FlatteningStratification`; shared root with `Quot-Foundations`)
- [ ] **Smooth proper curves** ‚Äî projectivity, normalization iso, function-field equivalence *(held: classically RR-dependent; Route C paused)*
- [ ] **Top goal: `Pic_{C/k}` representability + Jacobian = Albanese** *(once the substrate + engine themes close)*

## Cech-Cohomology  *(‚úÖ complete ‚Äî 0 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` ‚Äî for a separated quasi-compact `f : X ‚ü∂ S`,
a quasi-coherent `F`, and a finite affine open cover, the cohomology of the relative ƒåech
complex computes `R‚Å±f_* F`. Unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal).

- [x] **Combinatorial / free ƒåech engine** ‚Äî alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, exactness
- [x] **Section ƒåech complex & localization comparison** ‚Äî `AwayComparison`, `phi/phiL` naturality
- [x] **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing)** ‚Äî tilde-vanishing ‚áí affine ƒåech vanishing
- [x] **Cover/nerve combinatorics** ‚Äî ƒåech nerve, wide pullbacks, `pushPull` sigma iso, finitary-extensive distributivity
- [x] **Quasi-coherence on opens** ‚Äî over-equivalences, restrict-to-basic-open, modules-over-opens equivalence
- [x] **Higher direct image & acyclicity** ‚Äî injective resolutions, horseshoe lemma, pushforward acyclicity
- [x] **PushPull functoriality** ‚Äî `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, pentagon
- [x] **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`** *(proved iter-079, 0 sorries)*

## Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso  *(prover stage ‚Äî extraction hub ‚Üí Jacobian, 2 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** the comparison-isomorphism substrate giving `Pic‚ôØ_{C/k}` its abelian-group
structure (the A.1.c.sub package; merges back into the Jacobian challenge).

- [x] **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery** ‚Äî `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` **sorry-free**
- [x] **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route)** ‚Äî `TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport` **sorry-free**
- [x] **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor** ‚Äî `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor` **sorry-free**
- [~] **Tensor unitors & Picard-group assembly** ‚Äî `TensorObjSubstrate` (PicGroup/picCommGroup wiring), **2 residual `sorry`**

## Quot-Foundations  *(prover stage ‚Äî 21 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** the ƒåech-independent (i = 0) leg of FGA Picard representability ‚Äî flat base
change, generic flatness, and Quot/Grassmannian foundations. The Grassmannian-quotient
representability endgame and the flat-base-change/SNAP legs are carved into the sibling
extractions `GR-quot_closure` and `FBC-B_SNAP-chain` (below); proofs merge back here.

- [x] **Grassmannian construction & gluing** ‚Äî `GrassmannianCells`, `GlueDescent` **sorry-free** (rank-quotient setoid, charts, transition cocycle, effective descent)
- [x] **RelativeSpec / flattening stratification** ‚Äî `RelativeSpec`, `FlatteningStratification` **sorry-free**
- [x] **Graded Hilbert‚ÄìSerre helper** ‚Äî `GradedHilbertSerre`, `RegroupHelper` **sorry-free**
- [~] **Flat base change (degree 0)** ‚Äî `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange` (√ó4), `FlatBaseChangeGlobal` (√ó1); pushforward Mayer‚ÄìVietoris / finite-generation criteria
- [~] **Tautological / universal quotient** ‚Äî `GrassmannianQuot` (√ó3): `represents` done, `tautologicalQuotient_epi` closing
- [~] **Quot scheme** ‚Äî `QuotScheme` (√ó4): `RepresentableBy` upgrade + Quot-representability core
- [~] **Section graded ring (SNAP)** ‚Äî `Picard/SectionGradedRing` (√ó9): cast coherence ‚Üí Hilbert polynomial *(shared with the sibling extractions)*

## GR-quot_closure  *(prover stage ‚Äî 13 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** representability of the relative Grassmannian ‚Äî the ƒåech-independent (H‚Å∞) leg that
builds `Grass(V, d)` from affine charts via the `GL_d` cocycle and proves it represents the
rank-`d`-quotient functor. Extracted from `Quot-Foundations`; merges back as a three-way merge.

- [x] **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent** ‚Äî `GrassmannianCells`, `GrassmannianQuot`, `GlueDescent`, `GradedHilbertSerre`, `RelativeSpec` **sorry-free**
- [~] **Quot scheme** ‚Äî `QuotScheme` (√ó4): representability endgame
- [~] **Section graded ring (SNAP)** ‚Äî `Picard/SectionGradedRing` (√ó9) *(shared with `FBC-B_SNAP-chain`)*

## FBC-B_SNAP-chain  *(prover stage ‚Äî 14 open `sorry`)*

**Goal:** the flat-base-change (FBC-B) leg of the Quot/Picard-representability cone, sharing the
SNAP section-graded-ring foundation with `GR-quot_closure`. *(Lean scaffolding has been
generated ‚Äî this is no longer an empty extraction skeleton.)*

- [x] **Regroup helper** ‚Äî `Cohomology/RegroupHelper` **sorry-free**
- [~] **Flat base change (FBC-B)** ‚Äî `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange` (√ó4), `FlatBaseChangeGlobal` (√ó4): pushforward flat base-change leg
- [~] **Section graded ring (SNAP)** ‚Äî `Picard/SectionGradedRing` (√ó6): shared foundation with `GR-quot_closure`

---

## Related papers  *(üìù blueprint stage ‚Äî LaTeX blueprint written, Lean formalization not begun)*

All 26 related-paper projects below are blueprint-only: their Lean targets are stub
aggregators (0 real declarations). The checklist items track the **blueprint chapters**;
every Lean item is therefore `[ ]`.

### A. Papers that consume the AG base

#### MR4213770 ‚Äî Universal secant bundles and syzygies of canonical curves  ¬∑  needs Jacobian + ƒåech + Quot
**Main theorem:** generic Green's conjecture for general canonical curves (`K_{k,1}(C,œâC)=0` for general genus 2k/2k+1) and the even-genus geometric-syzygy structure theorem for K3 sections.

- [ ] **Peer dependencies** ‚Äî R‚Å±f_*, H^i scheme cohomology, pushforward base change, stalkwise module-iso *(explicit anchors)*
- [ ] **Universal secant bundles & syzygies** ‚Äî universal zero locus, secant bundles, local freeness, Voisin's theorem, even-genus structure, geometric syzygy conjecture

#### MR4228499 ‚Äî Bounds for the stalks of perverse sheaves in characteristic p  ¬∑  needs Jacobian + ƒåech
**Main theorem:** the Massey stalk bound (stalk dim ‚â§ polar multiplicity of the characteristic cycle) and the Shende‚ÄìTsimerman Betti-number bound for theta-locus intersections in hyperelliptic Jacobians.

- [ ] **Peer dependencies** ‚Äî Jacobian, Weil divisors, symmetric powers C‚Åø, canonical differentials, R‚Å±f_*
- [ ] **Terminology & axioms** ‚Äî conical cycles, polar multiplicities, transversality; nearby/vanishing cycles, singular support, characteristic cycles (as black boxes)
- [ ] **Massey bound** ‚Äî polar-multiplicity equivalences, global polar bound
- [ ] **Shende‚ÄìTsimerman application** ‚Äî theta-map characteristic cycle, pushforward decomposition, explicit bound

#### MR4276287 ‚Äî Uniformity in Mordell‚ÄìLang for curves  ¬∑  needs Jacobian
**Main theorem:** uniform bound `c^{1+œÅ}` on rational points of genus-`g` curves over number fields of bounded degree (œÅ = Mordell‚ÄìWeil rank of the Jacobian).

- [ ] **Moduli space & Jacobian** ‚Äî moduli stack of curves, Jacobian abelian variety, principal polarization, Torelli / universal family
- [ ] **Heights & positivity** ‚Äî Weil heights on varieties and abelian groups, positivity conditions
- [ ] **Arithmetic B√©zout & uniform bounds** ‚Äî intersection-theoretic height estimates, Northcott, R√©mond bound, Raynaud‚ÄìManin‚ÄìMumford, torsion-packet bounds

#### MR4433079 ‚Äî Intersection complexes and unramified L-factors  ¬∑  needs AG base (aspirational)
**Main theorem:** for type-T affine spherical varieties, the IC function on the arc space equals a ratio of local unramified L-values.

- [ ] **Group data & arc spaces** ‚Äî reductive data, type-T condition, arc spaces, IC functions
- [ ] **Global models & central fibers** ‚Äî Zastava spaces, spectral curves, Mirkoviƒá‚ÄìVilonen cycles, Kashiwara crystal structure
- [ ] **Semi-smallness & dimension estimates** ‚Äî semi-smallness of Zastava maps, central-fiber dimension bounds
- [ ] **IC formula & asymptotics** ‚Äî nearby-cycle commutation, affine-closure formula, Plancherel decomposition

#### MR4665779 ‚Äî A Chabauty‚ÄìColeman bound for surfaces  ¬∑  conceptually needs Jacobian / abelian varieties
**Main theorem:** the Caro‚ÄìPast√©n Chabauty‚ÄìColeman bound for hyperbolic surfaces in abelian varieties of Mordell‚ÄìWeil rank ‚â§ 1, with rational/quadratic-points applications.

- [ ] **Geometry interfaces & packages** ‚Äî curves and surface singularities, abelian varieties and specialization
- [ ] **Local Chabauty‚ÄìColeman bound** ‚Äî bound over Q_p extensions, specialization to Q_p
- [ ] **Number-field transfer & applications** ‚Äî rational points, density-one and symmetric-square applications

### B. Self-contained blueprint formalizations  *(no AG-base dependency)*

#### MR4199442 ‚Äî Standard conjectures for abelian fourfolds
**Main theorem:** the standard conjecture of Hodge type for abelian fourfolds in characteristic p (positive signature of the codimension-2 numerical intersection form).

- [ ] **Motives & realizations** ‚Äî conventions, Chow‚ÄìK√ºnneth, Lefschetz formalism, CM structures
- [ ] **Exotic classes & quadratic forms** ‚Äî exotic motives in H‚Å¥, rank-two orthogonal motives, Hilbert symbol
- [ ] **p-adic periods & Clozel** ‚Äî Hyodo‚ÄìKato realization, crystalline periods, num = ‚Ñì-adic hom
- [ ] **Main theorem** ‚Äî standard conjecture of Hodge type

#### MR4258055 ‚Äî A refined Brill‚ÄìNoether theory over Hurwitz spaces
**Main theorem:** for general degree-`k` genus-`g` covers, the Brill‚ÄìNoether splitting locus is smooth of pure dimension `g ‚àí u(‚Éóe)` (empty when `u > g`).

- [ ] **Splitting types & loci** ‚Äî bundles on ‚Ñô¬π, balanced bundles, expected codimension, degeneracy loci
- [ ] **Degeneration & smoothness** ‚Äî elliptic pushforward, deformation-theoretic smoothness, Hurwitz conditions

#### MR4372220 ‚Äî Anticyclotomic Iwasawa theory at Eisenstein primes
**Main theorem:** structure of anticyclotomic Selmer groups / Heegner‚ÄìHoward‚ÄìKolyvagin systems for rational elliptic curves at Eisenstein primes.

- [ ] **Local Iwasawa & Selmer** ‚Äî local Iwasawa theory, Selmer groups
- [ ] **Algebraic / analytic comparison** ‚Äî algebraic and analytic Iwasawa comparison
- [ ] **Howard‚ÄìKolyvagin & main conjecture** ‚Äî Kolyvagin construction, main-conjecture applications

#### MR4411733 ‚Äî Very stable Higgs bundles, equivariant multiplicity and mirror symmetry
**Main theorem:** classification of very stable Higgs bundles (Bia≈Çynicki-Birula theory), the multiplicity formula, and mirror-symmetry (Fourier‚ÄìMukai) isomorphisms.

- [ ] **Classification & BB partition** ‚Äî very stable Higgs bundles, weight decomposition
- [ ] **Multiplicity & mirror symmetry** ‚Äî multiplicity formula, mirror bundle / Fourier‚ÄìMukai, equivariant Euler pairing

#### MR4413746 ‚Äî Rigid local systems and the multiplicative eigenvalue problem
**Main theorem:** bijection between rigid irreducible unitary local systems on ‚Ñô¬π and F-vertices of the multiplicative eigenvalue polytope; no rigid irreducibles of rank > 1 when n is prime.

- [ ] **Rigid local systems & polytope** ‚Äî irreducibility, multiplicative eigenvalue vertices
- [ ] **Parabolic parametrization & duality** ‚Äî F-line bundles on parabolic bundles, strange duality
- [ ] **Schubert calculus & asymptotics** ‚Äî quantum Schubert calculus, GW inequalities, nearby cycles

#### MR4419629 ‚Äî Squarefree values of polynomial discriminants I
**Main theorem:** the density of monic integer polynomials with squarefree discriminant exists and equals an explicit Euler product; maximal-order density is Œ∂(2)‚Åª¬π.

- [ ] **Densities & local integrals** ‚Äî height/discriminant definitions, p-adic local densities
- [ ] **Invariant theory & orbit counting** ‚Äî SO_n invariant theory, geometry-of-numbers (odd/even degree)
- [ ] **Main density theorems** ‚Äî squarefree-discriminant and maximal-order densities, monogenic fields

#### MR4433080 ‚Äî Hitchin fibrations, abelian surfaces and the P=W conjecture
**Main theorem:** P=W for genus-2 curves (all rank); the conjecture on the even tautological subalgebra in all genera.

- [ ] **Setup & filtrations** ‚Äî Hitchin fibration, character variety, NAH diffeomorphism, weight/perverse filtrations
- [ ] **Tautological classes & perverse sheaves** ‚Äî twisted Chern character, perverse truncation/splitting
- [ ] **Abelian surfaces & genus-2 result** ‚Äî Hilbert‚ÄìChow, Markman monodromy, even/odd tautological perversity

#### MR4448992 ‚Äî Geometric Bogomolov conjecture in arbitrary characteristics
**Main theorem:** small-points-dense subvarieties of abelian varieties over function fields are special (translate of an abelian subvariety by a torsion point).

- [ ] **Heights & small points** ‚Äî naive/canonical heights, special subvarieties, K/k-trace
- [ ] **Non-proper intersections** ‚Äî Chow groups, Bertini, excess locus, comparison inequality
- [ ] **Descent & reduction** ‚Äî lowering transcendence degree, Yamaki reduction, Manin‚ÄìMumford application

#### MR4493324 ‚Äî The universal p-adic Gross‚ÄìZagier formula
**Main theorem:** the p-adic height of the universal Heegner class over a Hida family equals the cyclotomic derivative of the p-adic L-function (with classical-point specializations).

- [ ] **Setup & Galois representations** ‚Äî Shimura varieties, automorphic reps, Bloch‚ÄìKato/Nekov√°≈ô Selmer complexes
- [ ] **Height pairing & Heegner classes** ‚Äî p-adic height pairing, Heegner cycles, universal class over Hida families
- [ ] **Main theorems** ‚Äî p-adic BBK, Gross‚ÄìZagier, universal-class interpolation

#### MR4513142 ‚Äî There is no Enriques surface over the integers
**Main theorem:** the moduli stack of Enriques surfaces over Spec(‚Ñ§) is empty (no flat proper family over ‚Ñ§).

- [ ] **Picard schemes & Enriques surfaces** ‚Äî numerically trivial Picard scheme, exceptional vs non-exceptional
- [ ] **Families & reduction to ‚Ñ§** ‚Äî constant Picard scheme, Minkowski/Brauer vanishing, canonical coverings
- [ ] **Nonexceptional classification** ‚Äî Weierstrass/Jacobian fibrations, elimination of Kodaira symbols

#### MR4583777 ‚Äî Tate's thesis in the de Rham setting
**Main theorem:** a canonical DG equivalence `D^!(LLA) ‚âÖ IndCohStar(Y)` between sheaves on the algebraic loop space and ind-coherent sheaves on a moduli of rank-1 de Rham local systems.

- [ ] **Loop spaces & moduli Y** ‚Äî loop space LLA, rank-1 de Rham local systems, derived equalizer / gauge quotient
- [ ] **Ind-coherent sheaves** ‚Äî Coh/IndCohStar, t-structure, semi-coherence
- [ ] **Spectral realization & equivalence** ‚Äî Weyl-algebra convolution, main DG equivalence

#### MR4628606 ‚Äî Pixton's formula and Abel‚ÄìJacobi theory on the Picard stack
**Main theorem:** Pixton's formula computes the universal twisted double-ramification cycle on the Picard stack and vanishes above codimension g.

- [ ] **DR cycles & prestable graphs** ‚Äî double ramification cycles, decorated graphs
- [ ] **Pixton's formula & vanishing** ‚Äî modular construction, vanishing above codim g
- [ ] **Twisted differentials & universal formula** ‚Äî Farkas‚ÄìPandharipande Conjecture A, invariance, degree-0 universal formula
